## GIM ↔ NIST Crosswalk



### Building the GIM ↔ NIST Crosswalk

This is the Week 5 deliverable: mapping your GIM Framework's 58 nodes across 5 pillars to NIST AI RMF's 4 functions and subcategories.

<CodeBlock b64="" />

<AnalogyBox emoji="🔗" title="Standards Mapping = Universal Translator">
The crosswalk is like a translation layer between two governance languages. When a CISO asks "How does your framework handle NIST GOVERN 5?", you point to GIM Node 1.3 (Stakeholder Mapping). When a data governance lead asks "Where is bias testing in GIM?", you point to Node 3.2 and its NIST mapping. This bidirectional traceability is what makes GIM enterprise-credible.
</AnalogyBox>

<SeeItInRe3 text="The Gimmy chatbot on re3.live could be enhanced to answer 'Which GIM node maps to NIST MEASURE 2?' — making the crosswalk interactive." />
